title: Afeyan, Nagler & Tu (2025): Spatial RCC analysis: Phenotype enrichment of clones in the TLS vs outside the TLS
text:
Introduction
This is a book that contains all code to reproduce figures related to spatial analysis of TCR data associated with Afeyan, Nagler & Tu, et al (2025)
Note that while the code is identical, this book was rendered after final manuscript figures were generated and thus minor differences may arise, particularly for steps that are non-deterministic or rely on random number generators.

title: Afeyan, Nagler & Tu (2025): Spatial RCC analysis: Phenotype enrichment of clones in the TLS vs outside the TLS
section: Tertiary lymphoid structures are reservoirs of stem-like tumor-specific T cells in renal cell carcinoma
text:
Tertiary lymphoid structures are reservoirs of stem-like tumor-specific T cells in renal cell carcinoma
Alexander B. Afeyan†, Adi Nagler†, Chloe R. Tu†, Gabriel Roberti De Oliveira, Berkay Simsek, Maxwell D. Seager, Nourhan El Ahmar, Haley E. Sax, Emma Lin, Amit Sud, Mehdi Borji, Cleo Forman, Sophia Liu, Patrick A. Ott, Toni K. Choueiri, Jennifer G. Abelin, Richard Burack, Shuqiang Li, Kenneth J. Livak, Svitlana Tyekucheva, Derin B. Keskin, Fei Chen, Michael B. Atkins, Jeremy M. Simon, Sabina Signoretti, Giacomo Oliveira°, David A. Braun‡°, Catherine J. Wu‡°

Abstract
Tertiary lymphoid structures (TLS) are associated with improved response to immune checkpoint blockade across solid tumors1–3, but how they impact the phenotypic properties of tumor-specific T cells remains unknown. Across 24 treatment-naïve renal cell carcinoma (RCC) tumors, TLS-containing tumors were more heavily infiltrated by exhausted CD8+ T cells with a reduced terminal exhaustion transcriptional program compared to TLS- tumors. Specificity screening of 554 T cell clonotypes expanded within the microenvironment of 6 RCC tumors revealed 82 TCRs reactive against tumor cells and/or RCC antigens. A subset of tumor-specific T cell clonotypes (12%) was enriched within TLS, and these expressed an increased program of stem-like progenitor exhaustion, associated with favorable anti-tumor immunity. However, in 60 independent RCC tumors, macrophages within tumor margins of TLS-containing tumors bore an inferred immunosuppressive phenotype, and were colocalized with exhausted putative tumor-reactive T cells in a subgroup that was further analyzed, thus supporting this mode of immune evasion as a counterbalance to T cell immune pressure. Our data reveal TLS as reservoirs of tumor-specific T cells with stem-like progenitor features that could be leveraged by T cell immunotherapies.

5.1 Add a generalized column “tls_status”

add_TLS_column <- function(obj){
  obj <- obj %>%
    mutate(tls_status = case_when(area %in% c("TLS1", "TLS2", "TLS3", "TLS4", "M2") ~ "inTLS",
                              area %in% c("Boundary", "Tumor"
                                          ) ~ "outTLS"))
  
  return(obj)
}

P102TA_10_RNA@meta.data <- P102TA_10_RNA@meta.data %>%
  add_TLS_column()
P102TA_33_RNA@meta.data <- P102TA_33_RNA@meta.data %>%
  add_TLS_column()

P108TA_26_RNA@meta.data <- P108TA_26_RNA@meta.data %>%
  add_TLS_column()
P108TA_29_RNA@meta.data <- P108TA_29_RNA@meta.data %>%

section: 15.1 Format raw GSVA enrichment scores from escape
text:
15.1 Format raw GSVA enrichment scores from escape

raw_dat <- as.data.frame(TLS_pucks@assays$escape.GSVA$data) %>%
  t() %>%
  as.data.frame()

# Remove "-"s and remove spaces from data and from gene sets
names(new_gene_sets) <- str_replace_all(names(new_gene_sets), "_", "")

colnames(raw_dat) <- str_replace_all(names(raw_dat), "-", "")

# Get dataframe
raw_md <- raw_dat %>%
  rownames_to_column("barcode") %>%
  full_join(TLS_pucks@meta.data, by = "barcode") %>%
  mutate(tls_status = factor(tls_status))

raw_md$tls_status <- relevel(raw_md$tls_status, ref = "outTLS")

# Select columns to use
raw_md <- raw_md[,c(1:3, 4, 37, 40, 43)]


15.1.1 Plot TCRs

15.1.1.1 Get means, SD and SE

sd.df <- raw_md %>%
  # aggregate by puck
  group_by(tls_status, shared_tex_id, orig.ident, Patient) %>% 
  summarize(mean_coret_p = round(mean(coret), 8),
            mean_tpex_p = round(mean(projecTILs.CD8.TPEX), 8)) %>%
  # aggregate across pucks
  group_by(tls_status, shared_tex_id, Patient) %>%
  summarize(n = n(),
            sd_coret = round(sd(mean_coret_p), 8),
            sd_tpex = round(sd(mean_tpex_p), 8),
            se_coret = round(sd_coret/sqrt(n), 8),
            se_tpex = round(sd_tpex/sqrt(n), 8),
            mean_coret = round(mean(mean_coret_p), 8),
            mean_tpex = round(mean(mean_tpex_p), 8)) %>%
  ungroup()

`summarise()` has grouped output by 'tls_status', 'shared_tex_id',
'orig.ident'. You can override using the `.groups` argument.

text:
18.1 Get permuted test statistic

# Get permuted test statistic for each gene set
model_perm <- vector("list", length(new_gene_sets))
names(model_perm) <- names(new_gene_sets)

n_perm <- 1000

# Count non-convergences
non_convergences <- vector("list", length(new_gene_sets))
names(non_convergences) <- names(new_gene_sets)

# Count times tested
tested <- vector("list", length(new_gene_sets))
names(tested) <- names(new_gene_sets)

# Loop across each gene set
for (gs in names(new_gene_sets)) {
  n_run <- 1
  n_saved <- 1
  non_conv <- 0
  
  print(gs)
  t_val_perm.vec <- c()
  # Do 1000 converging loops
  while(n_saved <= n_perm){
    # Print every saved 100 perms
    if (n_saved %% 100 == 0) { print(n_saved) }
    # Get the permuted replicate
    md_rep <- md_perm %>%
      filter(replicate == n_run)
    # Set runs as good
    bad_run <- FALSE
    # Attempt the model
    model <- withCallingHandlers(
      expr = {
        # Build formula with permutated TLs status
        f <- as.formula(paste(gs, "~ perm_tls_status + (1 | Patient/orig.ident)"))
        # Build linear model on permuted data
        lmer(f, data = md_rep, REML = TRUE)},
      warning = function(w) {
      # Note if the model can't converge
      bad_run <<- TRUE
      non_conv <<- non_conv + 1
      n_run <<- n_run + 1
      print("Couldn't converge")
      invokeRestart("muffleWarning")
      }
    )
      
    # Save the t val from the model if it has no warning messages
    if(!bad_run){
      # Get and save t val
      t_val_perm <- coef(summary(model))["perm_tls_statusinTLS","t value"]
      t_val_perm.vec <- c(t_val_perm.vec, t_val_perm)
      # Count that we've saved a run
      n_saved <- n_saved + 1
      n_run <- n_run + 1
    } else {
      print("Skipping model")
    }
  }
  model_perm[[gs]] <- t_val_perm.vec
  non_convergences[[gs]] <- non_conv
}

section: 14.1 Format raw GSVA enrichment scores from escape
text:
14.1 Format raw GSVA enrichment scores from escape

raw_dat <- as.data.frame(TLS_pucks@assays$escape.GSVA$data) %>%
  t() %>%
  as.data.frame()

# Remove "-"s and remove spaces from data and from gene sets
# names(new_gene_sets) <- str_replace_all(names(new_gene_sets), "-", "")
names(new_gene_sets) <- str_replace_all(names(new_gene_sets), "_", "")

colnames(raw_dat) <- str_replace_all(names(raw_dat), "-", "")

# Get dataframe
raw_md <- raw_dat %>%
  rownames_to_column("barcode") %>%
  full_join(TLS_pucks@meta.data, by = "barcode") %>%
  mutate(tls_status_gid = factor(tls_status_gid),
         final_neoant_id = factor(final_neoant_id))

raw_md$tls_status_gid <- relevel(raw_md$tls_status_gid, ref = "outTLS")

# # Select columns to use
raw_md <- raw_md[,c(1:4, 41, 44, 46)]


14.1.1 Plot TCRs

14.1.1.1 Get means, SD and SE

sd.df <- raw_md %>%
  # aggregate by puck
  group_by(tls_status_gid, final_neoant_id, orig.ident) %>% 
  summarize(mean_coret_p = round(mean(coret), 8),
            mean_tpex_p = round(mean(projecTILs.CD8.TPEX), 8)) %>%
  # aggregate across pucks
  group_by(tls_status_gid, final_neoant_id) %>%
  summarize(n = n(),
            sd_coret = round(sd(mean_coret_p), 8),
            sd_tpex = round(sd(mean_tpex_p), 8),
            se_coret = round(sd_coret/sqrt(n), 8),
            se_tpex = round(sd_tpex/sqrt(n), 8),
            mean_coret = round(mean(mean_coret_p), 8),
            mean_tpex = round(mean(mean_tpex_p), 8)) %>%
  ungroup()

`summarise()` has grouped output by 'tls_status_gid', 'final_neoant_id'. You
can override using the `.groups` argument.
`summarise()` has grouped output by 'tls_status_gid'. You can override using
the `.groups` argument.




14.1.1.2 Pivot for plots

long_mean.df <- sd.df %>%
  select(tls_status_gid, final_neoant_id, contains("mean")) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = !c(final_neoant_id, tls_status_gid), names_to = "signature", values_to = "score") %>%
  mutate(signature = str_replace(signature, "mean_", ""))

long_se.df <- sd.df %>%
  select(tls_status_gid, final_neoant_id, contains("se")) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = !c(final_neoant_id, tls_status_gid), names_to = "signature", values_to = "se") %>%
  mutate(signature = str_replace(signature, "se_", ""))

long_merged <- long_mean.df %>%
  full_join(long_se.df, by = c("final_neoant_id", "tls_status_gid", "signature")) %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(upper = score+se,
         lower = score-se,
         tls_status_gid = factor(tls_status_gid, levels = c("inTLS", "outTLS")))



14.1.1.3 Plot
Visually both the TPEX phenotype and core T cell phenotype are enriched inTLS

long_merged %>%
  filter(signature == "tpex") %>%
ggplot(aes(x = tls_status_gid, y = score)) +
  geom_line(aes(group = (final_neoant_id)), position=position_dodge(width=0.2)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin=lower, ymax=upper, group = final_neoant_id), width=.2, position=position_dodge(width=0.2), color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_point(aes(group = final_neoant_id, fill = tls_status_gid), color = "black",
             position=position_dodge(width=0.2), size = 3, shape = 22) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust=1)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#b2c4b9", "#d0b39e")) +
  ylab("Raw enrichment (GSVA)") +
  guides(color = "none") +
  ggtitle("TPEX enrichment values")







long_merged %>%
  filter(signature == "coret") %>%
ggplot(aes(x = tls_status_gid, y = score)) +
  geom_line(aes(group = (final_neoant_id)), position=position_dodge(width=0.2)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin=lower, ymax=upper, group = final_neoant_id), width=.2, position=position_dodge(width=0.2), color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_point(aes(group = final_neoant_id, fill = tls_status_gid), color = "black",
             position=position_dodge(width=0.2), size = 3, shape = 22) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust=1)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#b2c4b9", "#d0b39e")) +
  ylab("Raw enrichment (GSVA)") +
  guides(color = "none") +
  ggtitle("Core T enrichment values")

18.3 Get one sided pval for TPEX

tpex_obs_val <- df_obs %>%
  filter(gene_sets == "projecTILs.CD8.TPEX") %>%
  pull(obs_ts)
  
tpex_perm_val <- df_perm %>%
  filter(gene_sets == "projecTILs.CD8.TPEX") %>%
  pull(perm_ts)
  
# One sided
n_extreme <- length(tpex_perm_val[tpex_perm_val >= tpex_obs_val])
tpex_pval <- n_extreme/n_perm

# Replace 0 with small number
tpex_pval[tpex_pval == 0] <- 1/(n_perm)

tpex_pval
